title: Phytochemical Profiling of Dichloromethane Grain Extract of Digitaria exilis by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (LC-MS) and Its Pharmacological Relevance
author: Moses, Dele Adams
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Many medicinal plants are used traditionally, but their active principles are unknown  . Depending on the extraction solvent, medicinal plants contain myriads of phytochemicals and bioactive compounds, which are of pharmacognostic importance.   Studying the pharmacological   relevance   of plant extracts is essential   to discover new, effective, and safe therapeutic agents   (  drugs) for treating diseases , as they contain    useful phytoconstituents and   bioactive compounds.   This study therefore focused on the phytochemical profiling of  dichloromethane  grain extract of  Digitaria exilis  (DGEDE)   by  liquid chromatography coupled with mass spectrometry (LC-MS)  and the pharmacological relevance of the identified compound. The extraction, phytochemistry and LC-MS of DGEDE was carried out using standard protocol. Phytochemical screening of DGEDE revealed the presence of   alkaloid  s   (  2.57±0.0  3  ),   tannins   (  1  .  89  ±0.02),   phloba  tannin (  1  .47±0.0  1  ),   flavonoids   (2.  92  ±0.0  3  ),   saponins   (2.25±0.0  1  ),     phenols (2.17  ±  0.02),     cardiac glycosides (0.39  ±0.01  ), terpenoids   (  0  .7  8  ±0.01),   steroids (0.63  ±0.01  )   and anthraquinone (  1  .21±0.0  1  )  .   LC  -  MS   analysis   of   DGEGE,    via positive mode   ,   revealed   thirty-three   compounds  , with various base peak (m/z) and retention time (minute), including   buthylsopropylamine (114.233, 0.131) ;  2-butyl amino ethanol (116.123, 1.661);  ethylmethylsilane (74.054, 10.954); N-(Octadecanoyl)-sphing-4-enine (565.844, 12.748); N-hexadecyl-5-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (323.384, 17.676) and ethanolamine (61.132, 18.764) among others. The  compounds present in DGEDE were identified  based on their mass-to-charge (m/z) ratio and fragmentation pattern. The identified compound with specific peaks (compounds) is responsible for certain therapeutic effects. The pharmacological importance of the  dichloromethane  (DCM)  extract of  D. exilis  grain include purgative property, antidiabetic action, antioxidant potential, hepatoprotective benefit and lipid/obesity management ability.  The study provides information to bridge the traditional knowledge about medicinal plants with modern drug discovery and evidence-based medicine.
